questions.q: In what year was President John F. Kennedy assassinated?
Which Apollo mission first landed humans on the Moon in 1969?
Which legendary guitarist, who died in 1970, had Cherokee ancestry and set his Stratocaster on fire at Monterey Pop?
Roger Daltrey was the lead singer of which English rock band?
Who sang the signature version of 'My Way' in 1969, a song that became his anthem?
Who wrote the song 'Suzanne,' covered by many 60s artists?
In 1968, George Harrison's first solo album, often titled 'Wonderwall Music,' was the soundtrack to what?
Which country won the 1966 FIFA World Cup, held on home soil?
Which band released the 'Surrealistic Pillow' album (featuring 'White Rabbit') in 1967?
choices: Big Brother and the Holding Company

questions.q: At the 1965 Newport Folk Festival, which artist controversially performed with an electric guitar?
Who was the wild drummer of The Who, known for destroying drum kits onstage?
Jimmy Page left which 1960s band to form Led Zeppelin?
In 1969, which Canadian singer-songwriter wrote the song 'Woodstock'?
What was The Beatles' first #1 hit in the United States?
choices: I Want to Hold Your Hand

questions.q: What was the Rolling Stones' first #1 hit in the US, often shortened to '(I Can't Get No) ___'?
John Lennon and Yoko Ono released which anti-war anthem in 1969?
Who composed the famously sneaky theme music for the 1963 film 'The Pink Panther'?
Which band released 'Wild Thing' in 1966?
Who composed the primary songs for The Velvet Underground?
Which soulful Motown girl group released 'Stop! In the Name of Love' in 1965?
Which animated TV family, debuting in 1960, was the first prime-time cartoon and lived in the town of Bedrock?
The Beatles famously made their U.S. television debut in 1964 on which variety show?
Which large outdoor music festival took place on a dairy farm in upstate New York in August 1969?
Andy Warhol created a famous series of 1962 silkscreen paintings depicting cans of what product?
Which civil rights leader delivered the 'I Have a Dream' speech in Washington in 1963?
Which secret agent, played by Sean Connery, first appeared on screen in the 1962 film 'Dr. No'?
What dance craze, popularized by Chubby Checker, swept America in the early 1960s?
The 1965 film 'The Sound of Music' starred which actress as Maria?
Which sitcom featured a suburban witch named Samantha who twitched her nose to cast spells?
Which fashion model, nicknamed for her very slim figure, became an icon of 1960s 'mod' style?
Which 1969 'buddy' road movie starring Peter Fonda and Dennis Hopper became a counterculture landmark?
The British spy series 'The Avengers' paired John Steed with which leather-clad agent played by Diana Rigg?
Which Sci-Fi anthology series, hosted by Rod Serling, ended its original run in 1964?
Mary Quant, a London designer, is widely credited with popularizing which garment in the 1960s?
Which 1968 film by Stanley Kubrick featured a sentient computer named HAL 9000?
The 1967 'Summer of Love' was centered on which San Francisco neighborhood?
Which actor played Captain James T. Kirk in the original 'Star Trek' series that premiered in 1966?
The 1965 novel 'Dune' that became a sci-fi landmark was written by which author?
Which boxer changed his name from Cassius Clay after winning the heavyweight title in 1964?
The free 1969 festival at the Altamont Speedway, where the Rolling Stones headlined, ended in violence partly attributed to which group hired for security?
Timothy Leary, an advocate of psychedelics, popularized which famous 1960s counterculture phrase?
choices: Turn on, tune in, drop out

questions.q: Which folk-rock duo had a 1966 hit with 'The Sounds of Silence'?
The pop-art comic series and 1966 TV show 'Batman' famously starred which actor as the Caped Crusader?
Which iconic 1960s sports car, introduced by Ford in 1964, helped launch the 'pony car' segment?
Which 1968 Broadway rock musical, subtitled 'The American Tribal Love-Rock Musical,' shocked audiences with its nude scene and anti-war themes?
